{
  "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_01Gpwdu7DSr2ZTcmj1G2ntay",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n     2\u2192// Main Simulation Engine\n     3\u2192// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n     4\u2192//\n     5\u2192// Manages the game clock and drives the object engine + characters.\n     6\u2192// Ticks at configurable intervals (~5 seconds default).\n     7\u2192// Provides start/stop/pause controls and an event bus.\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192import { ObjectEngine } from './objects.js';\n    10\u2192import { OBJECT_DEFS, SECTORS, WORLD_TREE } from './world.js';\n    11\u2192import { Character, CHARACTER_DEFS, findInteractTile } from './characters.js';\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192export class SimulationEngine {\n    14\u2192  constructor(options = {}) {\n    15\u2192    this.tickIntervalMs = options.tickIntervalMs ?? 5000; // ~5 seconds\n    16\u2192    this.timeScale = options.timeScale ?? 1;              // speed multiplier\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192    this.objectEngine = new ObjectEngine();\n    19\u2192    this.running = false;\n    20\u2192    this.paused = false;\n    21\u2192    this.elapsedMs = 0;         // total simulation time\n    22\u2192    this.tickCount = 0;\n    23\u2192    this.listeners = new Map();  // event type \u2192 Set<callback>\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192    this._rafId = null;\n    26\u2192    this._lastTimestamp = null;\n    27\u2192    this._accumulator = 0;       // accumulated time toward next tick\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192    // Load world\n    30\u2192    this.objectEngine.addObjects(OBJECT_DEFS);\n    31\u2192    this.sectors = SECTORS;\n    32\u2192    this.worldTree = WORLD_TREE;\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192    // Load characters\n    35\u2192    this.characters = CHARACTER_DEFS.map(def => new Character(def));\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192    // AI integration\n    38\u2192    this.aiEngine = options.aiEngine || null;\n    39\u2192    this.aiMode = false;\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192    // Simulated clock \u2014 starts at 8:00 AM on Day 1\n    42\u2192    // Each tick advances 5 sim-minutes \u2192 1 sim-day per ~24 real minutes at 1x\n    43\u2192    this.simClock = {\n    44\u2192      day: 1,\n    45\u2192      minuteOfDay: 480,\n    46\u2192      simMinutesPerTick: 5,\n    47\u2192      get hour() { return Math.floor(this.minuteOfDay / 60); },\n    48\u2192      get minute() { return Math.floor(this.minuteOfDay % 60); },\n    49\u2192      get timeString() {\n    50\u2192        const h = this.hour;\n    51\u2192        const m = this.minute;\n    52\u2192        const period = h >= 12 ? 'PM' : 'AM';\n    53\u2192        const h12 = h % 12 || 12;\n    54\u2192        return `${h12}:${String(m).padStart(2, '0')} ${period}`;\n    55\u2192      },\n    56\u2192      get dayTimeString() {\n    57\u2192        return `Day ${this.day} \\u2014 ${this.timeString}`;\n    58\u2192      },\n    59\u2192      advance() {\n    60\u2192        this.minuteOfDay += this.simMinutesPerTick;\n    61\u2192        while (this.minuteOfDay >= 1440) {\n    62\u2192          this.minuteOfDay -= 1440;\n    63\u2192          this.day++;\n    64\u2192        }\n    65\u2192      },\n    66\u2192    };\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192    // Last notable event for the status bar\n    69\u2192    this.lastStatusText = '';\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192    // Forward object events to our bus\n    72\u2192    this.objectEngine.onTransition((event) => {\n    73\u2192      this._emit('object:transition', event);\n    74\u2192      this._reactToObjectEvent(event);\n    75\u2192    });\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192    // Attach AI engine\n    78\u2192    if (this.aiEngine) {\n    79\u2192      this.aiEngine.attach(this);\n    80\u2192    }\n    81\u2192  }\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192  start() {\n    84\u2192    if (this.running) return;\n    85\u2192    this.running = true;\n    86\u2192    this.paused = false;\n    87\u2192    this._lastTimestamp = performance.now();\n    88\u2192    this._accumulator = 0;\n    89\u2192    this._emit('engine:start', { elapsedMs: this.elapsedMs });\n    90\u2192    this._loop();\n    91\u2192  }\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192  stop() {\n    94\u2192    this.running = false;\n    95\u2192    this.paused = false;\n    96\u2192    if (this._rafId) {\n    97\u2192      cancelAnimationFrame(this._rafId);\n    98\u2192      this._rafId = null;\n    99\u2192    }\n   100\u2192    this._emit('engine:stop', { elapsedMs: this.elapsedMs, tickCount: this.tickCount });\n   101\u2192  }\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192  pause() {\n   104\u2192    if (!this.running) return;\n   105\u2192    this.paused = true;\n   106\u2192    this._emit('engine:pause', { elapsedMs: this.elapsedMs });\n   107\u2192  }\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192  resume() {\n   110\u2192    if (!this.running || !this.paused) return;\n   111\u2192    this.paused = false;\n   112\u2192    this._lastTimestamp = performance.now();\n   113\u2192    this._emit('engine:resume', { elapsedMs: this.elapsedMs });\n   114\u2192  }\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192  // Manually advance by a number of ticks (useful for testing)\n   117\u2192  manualTick(count = 1) {\n   118\u2192    const events = [];\n   119\u2192    for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {\n   120\u2192      const tickEvents = this.objectEngine.tick(this.tickIntervalMs);\n   121\u2192      this.tickCount++;\n   122\u2192      this.elapsedMs += this.tickIntervalMs;\n   123\u2192      this._wanderTick();\n   124\u2192      this._updateCharacters(this.tickIntervalMs);\n   125\u2192      events.push(...tickEvents);\n   126\u2192    }\n   127\u2192    this._emit('engine:tick', {\n   128\u2192      tickCount: this.tickCount,\n   129\u2192      elapsedMs: this.elapsedMs,\n   130\u2192      events,\n   131\u2192    });\n   132\u2192    return events;\n   133\u2192  }\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192  // Interact with an object (character-triggered)\n   136\u2192  interact(objectId, action = 'use') {\n   137\u2192    return this.objectEngine.interact(objectId, action);\n   138\u2192  }\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192  // Get object by id\n   141\u2192  getObject(objectId) {\n   142\u2192    return this.objectEngine.get(objectId);\n   143\u2192  }\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192  // Get all objects\n   146\u2192  getAllObjects() {\n   147\u2192    return this.objectEngine.getAll();\n   148\u2192  }\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192  // Get objects in a sector\n   151\u2192  getObjectsInSector(sector) {\n   152\u2192    return this.objectEngine.getBySector(sector);\n   153\u2192  }\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192  // Get all characters\n   156\u2192  getCharacters() {\n   157\u2192    return this.characters;\n   158\u2192  }\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192  // Full state snapshot\n   161\u2192  snapshot() {\n   162\u2192    return {\n   163\u2192      running: this.running,\n   164\u2192      paused: this.paused,\n   165\u2192      elapsedMs: this.elapsedMs,\n   166\u2192      tickCount: this.tickCount,\n   167\u2192      tickIntervalMs: this.tickIntervalMs,\n   168\u2192      timeScale: this.timeScale,\n   169\u2192      aiMode: this.aiMode,\n   170\u2192      simClock: { day: this.simClock.day, time: this.simClock.timeString },\n   171\u2192      objects: this.objectEngine.snapshot(),\n   172\u2192      characters: this.characters.map(c => ({\n   173\u2192        id: c.id, name: c.name, tx: c.tx, ty: c.ty,\n   174\u2192        action: c.action, facing: c.facing, mood: c.mood,\n   175\u2192        targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId,\n   176\u2192      })),\n   177\u2192    };\n   178\u2192  }\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Event bus \u2500\u2500\n   181\u2192\n   182\u2192  on(eventType, callback) {\n   183\u2192    if (!this.listeners.has(eventType)) {\n   184\u2192      this.listeners.set(eventType, new Set());\n   185\u2192    }\n   186\u2192    this.listeners.get(eventType).add(callback);\n   187\u2192    return () => this.off(eventType, callback);\n   188\u2192  }\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192  off(eventType, callback) {\n   191\u2192    this.listeners.get(eventType)?.delete(callback);\n   192\u2192  }\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192  _emit(eventType, data) {\n   195\u2192    const callbacks = this.listeners.get(eventType);\n   196\u2192    if (callbacks) {\n   197\u2192      for (const cb of callbacks) {\n   198\u2192        cb(data);\n   199\u2192      }\n   200\u2192    }\n   201\u2192    // Also emit wildcard\n   202\u2192    const wildcards = this.listeners.get('*');\n   203\u2192    if (wildcards) {\n   204\u2192      for (const cb of wildcards) {\n   205\u2192        cb({ type: eventType, ...data });\n   206\u2192      }\n   207\u2192    }\n   208\u2192  }\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Character AI / Wander behavior \u2500\u2500\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192  _wanderTick() {\n   213\u2192    // Advance simulated clock each tick\n   214\u2192    this.simClock.advance();\n   215\u2192\n   216\u2192    for (const char of this.characters) {\n   217\u2192      if (char.action !== 'idle') continue;\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192      // AI mode: delegate to AI decision engine\n   220\u2192      if (this.aiMode && this.aiEngine) {\n   221\u2192        if (!this.aiEngine.isPending(char.id)) {\n   222\u2192          this.aiEngine.tick(char, this.tickIntervalMs);\n   223\u2192        }\n   224\u2192        continue;\n   225\u2192      }\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192      // Random wander with cooldown\n   228\u2192      char.wanderCooldown -= this.tickIntervalMs;\n   229\u2192      if (char.wanderCooldown > 0) continue;\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192      // Priority actions first (reactive behavior), then random wander\n   232\u2192      const priority = this._getPriorityTarget(char);\n   233\u2192      const target = priority ? priority.obj : this._pickWanderTarget(char);\n   234\u2192      const action = priority ? priority.action : 'use';\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192      if (!target) {\n   237\u2192        char.wanderCooldown = 5000;\n   238\u2192        continue;\n   239\u2192      }\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192      const tile = findInteractTile(target.position);\n   242\u2192      if (!tile) {\n   243\u2192        char.wanderCooldown = 5000;\n   244\u2192        continue;\n   245\u2192      }\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192      // Already adjacent \u2014 interact directly\n   248\u2192      const atTarget = tile.tx === Math.round(char.tx) && tile.ty === Math.round(char.ty);\n   249\u2192      if (atTarget) {\n   250\u2192        this._doInteraction(char, target, action);\n   251\u2192        char.wanderCooldown = 30000 + Math.random() * 30000;\n   252\u2192      } else if (char.moveTo(tile.tx, tile.ty)) {\n   253\u2192        char.targetObjectId = target.id;\n   254\u2192        char.pendingAction = action;\n   255\u2192        char.wanderCooldown = 30000 + Math.random() * 30000;\n   256\u2192      } else {\n   257\u2192        char.wanderCooldown = 5000;\n   258\u2192      }\n   259\u2192    }\n   260\u2192  }\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192  /** Immediate random wander (no cooldown) \u2014 used as AI fallback. */\n   263\u2192  _randomWander(char) {\n   264\u2192    const priority = this._getPriorityTarget(char);\n   265\u2192    const target = priority ? priority.obj : this._pickWanderTarget(char);\n   266\u2192    const action = priority ? priority.action : 'use';\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192    if (!target) return;\n   269\u2192\n   270\u2192    const tile = findInteractTile(target.position);\n   271\u2192    if (!tile) return;\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192    const atTarget = tile.tx === Math.round(char.tx) && tile.ty === Math.round(char.ty);\n   274\u2192    if (atTarget) {\n   275\u2192      this._doInteraction(char, target, action);\n   276\u2192    } else if (char.moveTo(tile.tx, tile.ty)) {\n   277\u2192      char.targetObjectId = target.id;\n   278\u2192      char.pendingAction = action;\n   279\u2192    }\n   280\u2192  }\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192  _updateCharacters(deltaMs) {\n   283\u2192    for (const char of this.characters) {\n   284\u2192      const wasWalking = char.action === 'walking';\n   285\u2192      char.update(deltaMs);\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192      // Character just arrived at destination\n   288\u2192      if (wasWalking && char.action === 'idle') {\n   289\u2192        if (char.targetObjectId) {\n   290\u2192          const obj = this.objectEngine.get(char.targetObjectId);\n   291\u2192          if (obj) {\n   292\u2192            this._doInteraction(char, obj, char.pendingAction || 'use');\n   293\u2192          } else {\n   294\u2192            char.targetObjectId = null;\n   295\u2192          }\n   296\u2192          char.pendingAction = 'use';\n   297\u2192        } else if (char.targetCharId) {\n   298\u2192          // Character arrived near another character for conversation \u2014 face each other\n   299\u2192          const target = this.characters.find(c => c.id === char.targetCharId);\n   300\u2192          if (target) {\n   301\u2192            char._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);\n   302\u2192            target._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);\n   303\u2192          }\n   304\u2192          char.targetCharId = null;\n   305\u2192        }\n   306\u2192      }\n   307\u2192    }\n   308\u2192  }\n   309\u2192\n   310\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Object \u2192 interaction animation mapping \u2500\u2500\n   311\u2192  // Maps object ID substrings to the animation played during interaction.\n   312\u2192  static _getInteractAnim(objectId) {\n   313\u2192    if (!objectId) return null;\n   314\u2192    if (objectId.includes('book') || objectId.includes('desk') || objectId.includes('bookshelf')) {\n   315\u2192      return 'reading';\n   316\u2192    }\n   317\u2192    if (objectId.includes('bed') || objectId.includes('kotatsu') || objectId.includes('sofa') || objectId.includes('tv')) {\n   318\u2192      return 'sit';\n   319\u2192    }\n   320\u2192    // phone / general interaction fallback for everything else\n   321\u2192    return 'phone';\n   322\u2192  }\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192  _doInteraction(char, obj, action = 'use') {\n   325\u2192    // Face toward the object\n   326\u2192    const objCx = obj.position.tx + (obj.position.tw || 1) / 2;\n   327\u2192    const objCy = obj.position.ty + (obj.position.th || 1) / 2;\n   328\u2192    const dx = objCx - char.tx;\n   329\u2192    const dy = objCy - char.ty;\n   330\u2192    if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {\n   331\u2192      char._updateFacing(dx, 0);\n   332\u2192    } else {\n   333\u2192      char._updateFacing(0, dy);\n   334\u2192    }\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192    const event = this.objectEngine.interact(obj.id, action);\n   337\u2192    if (event) {\n   338\u2192      const anim = SimulationEngine._getInteractAnim(obj.id);\n   339\u2192      char.startInteraction(obj.id, anim);\n   340\u2192\n   341\u2192      // Successful interaction \u2192 happy (unless already upset)\n   342\u2192      if (char.mood !== 'upset') {\n   343\u2192        char.setMood('happy', 5000);\n   344\u2192      }\n   345\u2192\n   346\u2192      this._emit('character:interact', {\n   347\u2192        characterId: char.id, characterName: char.name,\n   348\u2192        objectId: obj.id, objectName: event.objectName,\n   349\u2192        from: event.from, to: event.to,\n   350\u2192        produces: event.produces || null,\n   351\u2192        emoji: event.emoji || null,\n   352\u2192      });\n   353\u2192    } else {\n   354\u2192      char.targetObjectId = null;\n   355\u2192    }\n   356\u2192  }\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192  _getPriorityTarget(char) {\n   359\u2192    // Sol: pick up book if closed and nearby\n   360\u2192    if (char.id === 'sol') {\n   361\u2192      const book = this.objectEngine.get('cafe_book');\n   362\u2192      if (book && book.state === 'closed') {\n   363\u2192        const dist = Math.abs(book.position.tx - char.tx) + Math.abs(book.position.ty - char.ty);\n   364\u2192        if (dist <= 6) {\n   365\u2192          return { obj: book, action: 'use' };\n   366\u2192        }\n   367\u2192      }\n   368\u2192    }\n   369\u2192\n   370\u2192    // Non-Maple near stocked display \u2192 take pastry\n   371\u2192    if (char.id !== 'maple') {\n   372\u2192      const display = this.objectEngine.get('cafe_display');\n   373\u2192      if (display && display.state === 'stocked') {\n   374\u2192        const dist = Math.abs(display.position.tx - char.tx) + Math.abs(display.position.ty - char.ty);\n   375\u2192        if (dist <= 8) {\n   376\u2192          return { obj: display, action: 'use' };\n   377\u2192        }\n   378\u2192      }\n   379\u2192    }\n   380\u2192\n   381\u2192    // Maple: restock empty display case\n   382\u2192    if (char.id === 'maple') {\n   383\u2192      const display = this.objectEngine.get('cafe_display');\n   384\u2192      if (display && display.state === 'empty') {\n   385\u2192        return { obj: display, action: 'use' };\n   386\u2192      }\n   387\u2192    }\n   388\u2192\n   389\u2192    return null;\n   390\u2192  }\n   391\u2192\n   392\u2192  _pickWanderTarget(char) {\n   393\u2192    const allObjects = this.objectEngine.getAll();\n   394\u2192\n   395\u2192    // 60% home sector, 40% any sector\n   396\u2192    let candidates;\n   397\u2192    if (Math.random() < 0.6) {\n   398\u2192      candidates = allObjects.filter(o => o.sector === char.homeSector);\n   399\u2192      if (candidates.length === 0) candidates = allObjects;\n   400\u2192    } else {\n   401\u2192      candidates = allObjects;\n   402\u2192    }\n   403\u2192\n   404\u2192    // Avoid repeating last interaction\n   405\u2192    if (char.lastObjectId && candidates.length > 1) {\n   406\u2192      candidates = candidates.filter(o => o.id !== char.lastObjectId);\n   407\u2192    }\n   408\u2192\n   409\u2192    if (candidates.length === 0) return null;\n   410\u2192    return candidates[Math.floor(Math.random() * candidates.length)];\n   411\u2192  }\n   412\u2192\n   413\u2192  _reactToObjectEvent(event) {\n   414\u2192    // Espresso done \u2192 Maple happy\n   415\u2192    if (event.objectId === 'cafe_espresso' && event.to === 'done') {\n   416\u2192      const maple = this.characters.find(c => c.id === 'maple');\n   417\u2192      if (maple) maple.setMood('happy', 10000);\n   418\u2192    }\n   419\u2192\n   420\u2192    // Easel finished \u2192 Nyx happy\n   421\u2192    if (event.objectId === 'apt_easel' && event.to === 'finished') {\n   422\u2192      const nyx = this.characters.find(c => c.id === 'nyx');\n   423\u2192      if (nyx) nyx.setMood('happy', 20000);\n   424\u2192    }\n   425\u2192\n   426\u2192    // Display restocked \u2192 nearby characters happy\n   427\u2192    if (event.objectId === 'cafe_display' && event.to === 'stocked') {\n   428\u2192      for (const char of this.characters) {\n   429\u2192        const display = this.objectEngine.get('cafe_display');\n   430\u2192        if (display) {\n   431\u2192          const dist = Math.abs(display.position.tx - char.tx) + Math.abs(display.position.ty - char.ty);\n   432\u2192          if (dist <= 6) char.setMood('happy', 10000);\n   433\u2192        }\n   434\u2192      }\n   435\u2192    }\n   436\u2192  }\n   437\u2192\n   438\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Internal loop \u2500\u2500\n   439\u2192\n   440\u2192  _loop() {\n   441\u2192    if (!this.running) return;\n   442\u2192\n   443\u2192    this._rafId = requestAnimationFrame((timestamp) => {\n   444\u2192      if (!this.running) return;\n   445\u2192\n   446\u2192      if (!this.paused) {\n   447\u2192        const rawDelta = timestamp - this._lastTimestamp;\n   448\u2192        const delta = Math.min(rawDelta, 500) * this.timeScale; // cap delta, apply scale\n   449\u2192\n   450\u2192        this._accumulator += delta;\n   451\u2192\n   452\u2192        // Process full ticks\n   453\u2192        while (this._accumulator >= this.tickIntervalMs) {\n   454\u2192          this._accumulator -= this.tickIntervalMs;\n   455\u2192          const tickEvents = this.objectEngine.tick(this.tickIntervalMs);\n   456\u2192          this.tickCount++;\n   457\u2192          this.elapsedMs += this.tickIntervalMs;\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192          this._wanderTick();\n   460\u2192\n   461\u2192          this._emit('engine:tick', {\n   462\u2192            tickCount: this.tickCount,\n   463\u2192            elapsedMs: this.elapsedMs,\n   464\u2192            events: tickEvents,\n   465\u2192          });\n   466\u2192        }\n   467\u2192\n   468\u2192        // Smooth character movement each frame\n   469\u2192        this._updateCharacters(delta);\n   470\u2192      }\n   471\u2192\n   472\u2192      this._lastTimestamp = timestamp;\n   473\u2192      this._loop();\n   474\u2192    });\n   475\u2192  }\n   476\u2192}\n   477\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

     1→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     2→// World Definition — Café Objects
     3→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     4→//
     5→// World tree: World → Sector → Arena → Object
     6→// Each object is a JSON state machine definition.
     7→// Times are in seconds (converted to ms by the engine).
     8→// Positions use tile coordinates (tx, ty) for the 16×24 grid.
     9→//
    10→// GROUND FLOOR (rows 10-20): Maple's café
    11→// UPPER FLOOR (rows 1-6): Nyx's apartment
    12→// Separator (rows 7-9): exterior/stairs
    13→
    14→// ── Sector layout ──
    15→export const SECTORS = {
    16→  cafe:      { name: 'Café',      tx: 8,  ty: 15 },
    17→  apartment: { name: 'Apartment', tx: 8,  ty: 4  },
    18→};

    22→  cafe: {

    24→    arenas: {
    25→      counter: {
    26→        name: 'Counter Area',
    27→        objects: ['cafe_espresso', 'cafe_coffee_maker', 'cafe_display', 'cafe_register', 'cafe_sink'],
    28→      },

    33→    },

    35→  apartment: {

    47→        name: 'Bedroom',

    53→

    55→
    56→export const OBJECT_DEFS = [
    57→  // ─── CAFÉ — Counter Area ───────────────────────────────
    58→
    59→  {
    60→    id: 'cafe_espresso',
    61→    name: 'espresso machine',
    62→    sector: 'cafe',
    63→    arena: 'counter',
    64→    position: { tx: 2, ty: 11 },
    65→    state: 'idle',
    66→    states: {
    67→      idle: {
    68→        visual: 'espresso_idle',
    69→        transitions: {
    70→          use: { next: 'brewing', requires: 'character' },
    71→        },
    72→      },
    73→      brewing: {
    74→        visual: 'espresso_brewing',
    75→        particles: 'small_steam',
    76→        auto_next: { state: 'done', after: 20 },
    77→      },
    78→      done: {
    79→        visual: 'espresso_done',
    80→        particles: 'steam',
    81→        emoji: '☕',

    83→        transitions: {
    84→          use: { next: 'idle' },
    85→        },
    86→      },
    87→    },
    88→  },
    89→
    90→  {
    91→    id: 'cafe_coffee_maker',
    92→    name: 'coffee maker',
    93→    sector: 'cafe',
    94→    arena: 'counter',

'\ud83d\udca4',\n   322\u2192        auto_next: { state: 'made', after: 120 },\n   323\u2192        transitions: {\n   324\u2192          use: { next: 'made' },\n   325\u2192        },\n   326\u2192      },\n   327\u2192    },\n   328\u2192  },\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192  {\n   331\u2192    id: 'apt_desk',\n   332\u2192    name: 'desk',\n   333\u2192    sector: 'apartment',\n   334\u2192    arena: 'bedroom',\n   335\u2192    position: { tx: 4, ty: 4 },\n   336\u2192    state: 'empty',\n   337\u2192    states: {\n   338\u2192      empty: {\n   339\u2192        visual: 'desk',\n   340\u2192        transitions: {\n   341\u2192          use: { next: 'working', requires: 'character' },\n   342\u2192        },\n   343\u2192      },\n   344\u2192      working: {\n   345\u2192        visual: 'desk',\n   346\u2192        emoji: '\u270f\ufe0f',\n   347\u2192        auto_next: { state: 'empty', after: 45 },\n   348\u2192        transitions: {\n   349\u2192          use: { next: 'empty' },\n SUCCESS 
Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the walk animation system. Let me provide a detailed analysis.

## Walk Animation Logic Summary

Based on my analysis of `/home/gem/project/src/characters.js` and `/home/gem/project/src/renderer.js`, here's how the walk animation system works:

### Character Movement and Animation State

**Walk Animation Initialization** (characters.js, lines 128-131):
- `walkFrame`: 0-3 (cycles through walk1, walk2, walk3, walk4)
- `walkTimer`: accumulates milliseconds between frame advances
- `walkFrameMs`: 200ms per frame (200ms * 4 frames = 800ms per full animation cycle)

**Movement State** (characters.js, lines 121-126):
- `path`: array of waypoint tile coordinates to follow
- `moveProgress`: 0-1 progress toward next waypoint
- `moveSpeed`: 2.0 tiles per second
- `prevTx, prevTy`: previous tile position for interpolation

### Animation Update Flow

**Per-frame Update** (characters.js, lines 199-247):

1. **Walk Animation Timing** (lines 201-206):
   ```javascript
   this.walkTimer += deltaMs;
   if (this.walkTimer >= this.walkFrameMs) {
     this.walkTimer -= this.walkFrameMs;
     this.walkFrame = (this.walkFrame + 1) % 4;
   }
   ```
   - Accumulates deltaMs and advances walkFrame every 200ms
   - Uses modulo 4 to cycle through frames 0, 1, 2, 3

2. **Movement Progress** (lines 208-223):
   - Calculates `tileDurationMs = 1000 / moveSpeed` (500ms per tile at 2.0 tiles/sec)
   - Increments `moveProgress` by `deltaMs / tileDurationMs`
   - When `moveProgress >= 1.0`:
     - Shifts to next waypoint in path
     - Updates `tx, ty, prevTx, prevTy` to arrived tile
     - Updates facing direction for next waypoint
     - Loops while moveProgress >= 1

3. **Arrival Detection** (lines 225-229):
   - When path is empty and moveProgress reset, action changes to 'idle'

**Sprite Selection** (characters.js, lines 149-158):
```javascript
get spriteKey() {
  if (this.action === 'walking') {
    return `${this.id}_walk${this.walkFrame + 1}`;  // walk1, walk2, walk3, walk4
  }
  if (this.action === 'interacting' && this.interactAnim) {
    return `${this.id}_${this.interactAnim}`;
  }
  return `${this.id}_idle`;
}
```

### Position Interpolation (Smooth Movement)

**Pixel Position Calculations** (characters.js, lines 169-181):
```javascript
get px() {
  if (this.path.length === 0) return this.tx * TILE_SIZE;
  const next = this.path[0];
  return (this.prevTx + (next.tx - this.prevTx) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;
}

get py() {
  if (this.path.length === 0) return this.ty * TILE_SIZE;
  const next = this.path[0];
  return (this.prevTy + (next.ty - this.prevTy) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;
}
```
- Interpolates between `prevTx/prevTy` and `next waypoint` using moveProgress (0-1)
- Multiplies by TILE_SIZE (48px) to get pixel coordinates

### Rendering

**Character Rendering** (renderer.js, lines 419-485):
- Reads `char.spriteKey` to get current sprite
- Applies idle bob effect (1px vertical wobble) when not walking
- Applies bounce scale (1.1→1.0) during first 100ms of interaction
- Characters sorted by `py` for depth ordering

### Potential Bugs and Stuttering Issues

I've identified several potential sources of animation stuttering or freezing:

1. **Delta Time Capping** (engine.js, line 448):
   ```javascript
   const delta = Math.min(rawDelta, 500) * this.timeScale;
   ```
   - If frame time exceeds 500ms, it's capped, which could cause frame skipping

2. **Walk Frame Accumulation Issue** (characters.js, lines 202-206):
   - If `deltaMs` is very large (e.g., 100ms or more), the animation could skip ahead unpredictably
   - Example: if deltaMs = 300ms, walkTimer accumulates, but only ONE frame increment happens per call
   - This could cause the animation to appear to stutter if frames are irregular

3. **Movement Progress Race Condition** (characters.js, lines 212-223):
   - The `while` loop processes multiple waypoints if `moveProgress` is too high
   - However, if a frame drops heavily, `moveProgress` could jump significantly
   - The animation frame doesn't reset proportionally when jumping waypoints

4. **No Frame Synchronization** (characters.js, lines 201-206):
   - Walk frame animation advances independently from waypoint transitions
   - If a character moves very slowly or pauses mid-waypoint, walkFrame keeps advancing
   - This could desynchronize walking sprite from actual movement

5. **Critical Issue - Animation During Waypoint Transition**:
   - When `moveProgress >= 1.0` and character shifts to next waypoint:
     - `moveProgress -= 1.0` (line 213)
     - But `walkFrame` already advanced separately
     - If this happens during frame drops, animation could appear to "freeze" or jump frames
   - Example: moveProgress=1.5, it processes first waypoint, then moveProgress becomes 0.5, but walkFrame might be on frame 0 or 3 depending on timing

6. **Renderer Depth Sort** (renderer.js, line 425):
   ```javascript
   const sorted = [...characters].sort((a, b) => a.py - b.py);
   ```
   - This sorts every frame, which could cause visual flickering if two characters have similar py

### Summary

The walk animation logic is **generally sound** but has potential for **frame-time-dependent stuttering** due to:
- Irregular frame timing causing walk frames to advance unpredictably
- Waypoint transitions not synchronized with animation frames
- Delta time capping potentially causing dropped frames
- No protection against extremely large deltaMs values that could cause multiple frame advances

The most likely source of visible stuttering would be **irregular frame rates** causing `deltaMs` to vary wildly, making the walk cycle appear to skip frames or freeze momentarily when it should be smooth.

Accumulates deltaMs and advances walkFrame every 200ms\n   - Uses modulo 4 to cycle through frames 0, 1, 2, 3\n\n2. **Movement Progress** (lines 208-223):\n   - Calculates `tileDurationMs = 1000 / moveSpeed` (500ms per tile at 2.0 tiles/sec)\n   - Increments `moveProgress` by `deltaMs / tileDurationMs`\n   - When `moveProgress >= 1.0`:\n     - Shifts to next waypoint in path\n     - Updates `tx, ty, prevTx, prevTy` to arrived tile\n     - Updates facing direction for next waypoint\n     - Loops while moveProgress >= 1\n\n3. **Arrival Detection** (lines 225-229):\n   - When path is empty and moveProgress reset, action changes to 'idle'\n\n**Sprite Selection** (characters.js, lines 149-158):\n```javascript\nget spriteKey() {\n  if (this.action === 'walking') {\n    return `${this.id}_walk${this.walkFrame + 1}`;  // walk1, walk2, walk3, walk4\n  }\n  if (this.action === 'interacting' && this.interactAnim) {\n    return `${this.id}_${this.interactAnim}`;\n  }\n  return `${this.id}_idle`;\n}\n```\n\n### Position Interpolation (Smooth Movement)\n\n**Pixel Position Calculations** (characters.js, lines 169-181):\n```javascript\nget px() {\n  if (this.path.length === 0) return this.tx * TILE_SIZE;\n  const next = this.path[0];\n  return (this.prevTx + (next.tx - this.prevTx) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;\n}\n\nget py() {\n  if (this.path.length === 0) return this.ty * TILE_SIZE;\n  const next = this.path[0];\n  return (this.prevTy + (next.ty - this.prevTy) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;\n}\n```\n- Interpolates between `prevTx/prevTy` and `next waypoint` using moveProgress (0-1)\n- Multiplies by TILE_SIZE (48px) to get pixel coordinates\n\n### Rendering\n\n**Character Rendering** (renderer.js, lines 419-485):\n- Reads `char.spriteKey` to get current sprite\n- Applies idle bob effect (1px vertical wobble) when not walking\n- Applies bounce scale (1.1\u21921.0) during first 100ms of interaction\n- Characters sorted by `py` for depth ordering\n\n### Potential Bugs and Stuttering Issues\n\nI've identified several potential sources of animation stuttering or freezing:\n\n1. **Delta Time Capping** (engine.js, line 448):\n   ```javascript\n   const delta = Math.min(rawDelta, 500) * this.timeScale;\n   ```\n   - If frame time exceeds 500ms, it's capped, which could cause frame skipping\n\n2. **Walk Frame Accumulation Issue** (characters.js, lines 202-206):\n   - If `deltaMs` is very large (e.g., 100ms or more), the animation could skip ahead unpredictably\n   - Example: if deltaMs = 300ms, walkTimer accumulates, but only ONE frame increment happens per call\n   - This could cause the animation to appear to stutter if frames are irregular\n\n3. **Movement Progress Race Condition** (characters.js, lines 212-223):\n   - The `while` loop processes multiple waypoints if `moveProgress` is too high\n   - However, if a frame drops heavily, `moveProgress` could jump significantly\n   - The animation frame doesn't reset proportionally when jumping waypoints\n\n4. **No Frame Synchronization** (characters.js, lines 201-206):\n   - Walk frame animation advances independently from waypoint transitions\n   - If a character moves very slowly or pauses mid-waypoint, walkFrame keeps advancing\n   - This could desynchronize walking sprite from actual movement\n\n5. **Critical Issue - Animation During Waypoint Transition**:\n   - When `moveProgress >= 1.0` and character shifts to next waypoint:\n     - `moveProgress -= 1.0` (line 213)\n     - But `walkFrame` already advanced separately\n     - If this happens during frame drops, animation could appear to \"freeze\" or jump frames\n   - Example: moveProgress=1.5, it processes first waypoint, then moveProgress becomes 0.5, but walkFrame might be on frame 0 or 3 depending on timing\n\n6. **Renderer Depth Sort** (renderer.js, line 425):\n   ```javascript\n   const sorted = [...characters].sort((a, b) => a.py - b.py);\n   ```\n   - This sorts every frame, which could cause visual flickering if two characters have similar py\n\n### Summary\n\nThe walk animation logic is **generally sound** but has potential for **frame-time-dependent stuttering** due to:\n- Irregular frame timing causing walk frames to advance unpredictably\n- Waypoint transitions not synchronized with animation frames\n- Delta time capping potentially causing dropped frames\n- No protection against extremely large deltaMs values that could cause multiple frame advances\n\nThe most likely source of visible stuttering would be **irregular frame rates** causing `deltaMs` to vary wildly, making the walk cycle appear to skip frames or freeze momentarily when it should be smooth."

USERMESSAGE #34
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     2→// Character System — Movement, Perception, A* Pathfinding
     3→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     4→//
     5→// Characters have: id, name, personality, position, action state,
     6→// mood, energy, facing direction. They move between tiles using
     7→// A* pathfinding on the walkable tilemap.
     8→//
     9→// Walk animation alternates walk1/walk2 sprites while moving.
    10→// Perception system returns nearby objects and characters within radius.
    11→
    12→import { MAP, T, MAP_COLS, MAP_ROWS, TILE_SIZE } from './tilemap.js';
    13→
    14→// ── Walkable tiles (everything except walls, fences, water) ──
    15→const UNWALKABLE = new Set([T.WALL, T.WALL_TOP, T.COUNTER, T.COUNTER_L, T.COUNTER_R, T.WINDOW]);
    16→
    17→export function isWalkable(tx, ty) {
    18→  if (tx < 0 || tx >= MAP_COLS || ty < 0 || ty >= MAP_ROWS) return false;
    19→  return !UNWALKABLE.has(MAP[ty][tx]);
    20→}
    21→
    22→// ── A* Pathfinding ──
    23→// Returns array of {tx, ty} waypoints (excludes start, includes goal),
    24→// or null if no path found. Returns [] if already at goal.
    25→
    26→const NEIGHBORS = [[0, -1], [0, 1], [-1, 0], [1, 0]];
    27→
    28→export function findPath(sx, sy, gx, gy) {
    29→  if (!isWalkable(gx, gy)) return null;
    30→
    31→  const startKey = sy * MAP_COLS + sx;
    32→  const goalKey = gy * MAP_COLS + gx;
    33→  if (startKey === goalKey) return [];
    34→
    35→  const open = [startKey];
    36→  const cameFrom = new Map();
    37→  const gScore = new Map([[startKey, 0]]);
    38→  const fScore = new Map([[startKey, Math.abs(sx - gx) + Math.abs(sy - gy)]]);
    39→  const closed = new Set();
    40→
    41→  while (open.length > 0) {
    42→    // Find node with lowest fScore
    43→    let bestIdx = 0;
    44→    let bestF = fScore.get(open[0]) ?? Infinity;
    45→    for (let i = 1; i < open.length; i++) {
    46→      const f = fScore.get(open[i]) ?? Infinity;
    47→      if (f < bestF) { bestF = f; bestIdx = i; }
    48→    }
    49→
    50→    const current = open[bestIdx];
    51→    if (current === goalKey) {
    52→      const path = [];
    53→      let c = current;
    54→      while (cameFrom.has(c)) {
    55→        path.unshift({ tx: c % MAP_COLS, ty: Math.floor(c / MAP_COLS) });
    56→        c = cameFrom.get(c);
    57→      }
    58→      return path;
    59→    }
    60→
    61→    open.splice(bestIdx, 1);
    62→    closed.add(current);
    63→
    64→    const cx = current % MAP_COLS;
    65→    const cy = Math.floor(current / MAP_COLS);
    66→
    67→    for (const [dx, dy] of NEIGHBORS) {
    68→      const nx = cx + dx;
    69→      const ny = cy + dy;
    70→      if (!isWalkable(nx, ny)) continue;
    71→
    72→      const nKey = ny * MAP_COLS + nx;
    73→      if (closed.has(nKey)) continue;
    74→
    75→      const tentG = (gScore.get(current) ?? Infinity) + 1;
    76→      if (tentG < (gScore.get(nKey) ?? Infinity)) {
    77→        cameFrom.set(nKey, current);
    78→        gScore.set(nKey, tentG);
    79→        fScore.set(nKey, tentG + Math.abs(nx - gx) + Math.abs(ny - gy));
    80→        if (!open.includes(nKey)) open.push(nKey);
    81→      }
    82→    }
    83→  }
    84→
    85→  return null; // no path
    86→}
    87→
    88→// ── Find walkable tile adjacent to an object (for interaction) ──
    89→export function findInteractTile(objPosition) {
    90→  const { tx, ty, tw = 1, th = 1 } = objPosition;
    91→  const candidates = [];
    92→
    93→  for (let x = tx - 1; x <= tx + tw; x++) {
    94→    if (isWalkable(x, ty - 1)) candidates.push({ tx: x, ty: ty - 1 });
    95→    if (isWalkable(x, ty + th)) candidates.push({ tx: x, ty: ty + th });
    96→  }
    97→  for (let y = ty; y < ty + th; y++) {
    98→    if (isWalkable(tx - 1, y)) candidates.push({ tx: tx - 1, ty: y });
    99→    if (isWalkable(tx + tw, y)) candidates.push({ tx: tx + tw, ty: y });
   100→  }
   101→
   102→  if (candidates.length === 0) return null;
   103→  return candidates[Math.floor(Math.random() * candidates.length)];
   104→}
   105→
   106→// ── Character class ──
   107→
   108→export class Character {
   109→  constructor(def) {
   110→    this.id = def.id;
   111→    this.name = def.name;
   112→    this.personality = def.personality;
   113→    this.tx = def.tx;
   114→    this.ty = def.ty;
   115→    this.homeSector = def.sector;
   116→    this.energy = 100;
   117→    this.facing = 'down'; // up | down | left | right
   118→    this.action = 'idle'; // idle | walking | interacting
   119→    this.colors = def.colors; // fallback render colors
   120→
   121→    // Movement
   122→    this.path = [];          // waypoints to follow
   123→    this.moveProgress = 0;   // 0–1 progress toward next waypoint
   124→    this.moveSpeed = 2.0;    // tiles per second
   125→    this.prevTx = def.tx;
   126→    this.prevTy = def.ty;
   127→
   128→    // Walk animation (4-frame cycle: walk1→walk2→walk3→walk4)
   129→    this.walkFrame = 0;      // 0–3 → walk1/walk2/walk3/walk4
   130→    this.walkTimer = 0;
   131→    this.walkFrameMs = 200;  // ms between frame advances
   132→
   133→    // Wander AI
   134→    this.targetObjectId = null;
   135→    this.wanderCooldown = 2000 + Math.random() * 5000; // start soon
   136→    this.interactTimer = 0;
   137→
   138→    // Interaction tracking
   139→    this.lastObjectId = null;
   140→    this.pendingAction = 'use';
   141→    this.interactAnim = null;    // 'reading' | 'sit' | 'phone' | null (uses idle when null)
   142→    this.targetCharId = null;   // ID of a character to face toward upon arrival
   143→
   144→    // Mood system
   145→    this.mood = 'content';       // happy | content | neutral | upset
   146→    this.moodTimer = 0;          // ms until mood resets to content
   147→  }
   148→
   149→  /** Sprite key for the current animation frame. */
   150→  get spriteKey() {
   151→    if (this.action === 'walking') {
   152→      return `${this.id}_walk${this.walkFrame + 1}`;
   153→    }
   154→    if (this.action === 'interacting' && this.interactAnim) {
   155→      return `${this.id}_${this.interactAnim}`;
   156→    }
   157→    return `${this.id}_idle`;
   158→  }
   159→
   160→  get moodEmoji() {
   161→    switch (this.mood) {
   162→      case 'happy': return '😊';
   163→      case 'content': return '😌';
   164→      case 'upset': return '😤';
   165→      default: return null;
   166→    }
   167→  }
   168→
   169→  /** Pixel X position (interpolated between tiles for smooth movement). */
   170→  get px() {
   171→    if (this.path.length === 0) return this.tx * TILE_SIZE;
   172→    const next = this.path[0];
   173→    return (this.prevTx + (next.tx - this.prevTx) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;
   174→  }
   175→
   176→  /** Pixel Y position (interpolated between tiles for smooth movement). */
   177→  get py() {
   178→    if (this.path.length === 0) return this.ty * TILE_SIZE;
   179→    const next = this.path[0];
   180→    return (this.prevTy + (next.ty - this.prevTy) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;
   181→  }
   182→
   183→  /** Start moving toward a goal tile. Returns true if a path was found. */
   184→  moveTo(goalTx, goalTy) {
   185→    const path = findPath(Math.round(this.tx), Math.round(this.ty), goalTx, goalTy);
   186→    if (path && path.length > 0) {
   187→      this.path = path;
   188→      this.prevTx = Math.round(this.tx);
   189→      this.prevTy = Math.round(this.ty);
   190→      this.moveProgress = 0;
   191→      this.action = 'walking';
   192→      this._updateFacing(path[0].tx - this.prevTx, path[0].ty - this.prevTy);
   193→      return true;
   194→    }
   195→    return false;
   196→  }
   197→
   198→  /** Per-frame update: advance position and animation. */
   199→  update(deltaMs) {
   200→    if (this.action === 'walking' && this.path.length > 0) {
   201→      // Walk animation cycle (4 frames)
   202→      this.walkTimer += deltaMs;
   203→      if (this.walkTimer >= this.walkFrameMs) {
   204→        this.walkTimer -= this.walkFrameMs;
   205→        this.walkFrame = (this.walkFrame + 1) % 4;
   206→      }
   207→
   208→      // Advance position along path
   209→      const tileDurationMs = 1000 / this.moveSpeed;
   210→      this.moveProgress += deltaMs / tileDurationMs;
   211→
   212→      while (this.moveProgress >= 1 && this.path.length > 0) {
   213→        this.moveProgress -= 1;
   214→        const arrived = this.path.shift();
   215→        this.tx = arrived.tx;
   216→        this.ty = arrived.ty;
   217→        this.prevTx = arrived.tx;
   218→        this.prevTy = arrived.ty;
   219→
   220→        if (this.path.length > 0) {
   221→          this._updateFacing(this.path[0].tx - this.tx, this.path[0].ty - this.ty);
   222→        }
   223→      }
   224→
   225→      // Arrived at destination
   226→      if (this.path.length === 0) {
   227→        this.moveProgress = 0;
   228→        this.action = 'idle';
   229→      }
   230→    } else if (this.action === 'interacting') {
   231→      this.interactTimer -= deltaMs;
   232→      if (this.interactTimer <= 0) {
   233→        this.action = 'idle';
   234→        this.targetObjectId = null;
   235→        this.interactAnim = null;
   236→      }
   237→    }
   238→
   239→    // Decay mood back to content
   240→    if (this.moodTimer > 0) {
   241→      this.moodTimer -= deltaMs;
   242→      if (this.moodTimer <= 0) {
   243→        this.mood = 'content';
   244→        this.moodTimer = 0;
   245→      }
   246→    }
   247→  }
   248→
   249→  _updateFacing(dx, dy) {
   250→    if (dx > 0) this.facing = 'right';
   251→    else if (dx < 0) this.facing = 'left';
   252→    else if (dy > 0) this.facing = 'down';
   253→    else if (dy < 0) this.facing = 'up';
   254→  }
   255→
   256→  /** Enter interaction state (after arriving at an object).
   257→   *  @param {string} objectId - ID of the object being interacted with.
   258→   *  @param {string|null} anim - Animation to play: 'reading' | 'sit' | 'phone' | null.
   259→   */
   260→  startInteraction(objectId, anim = null) {
   261→    this.action = 'interacting';
   262→    this.targetObjectId = objectId;
   263→    this.lastObjectId = objectId;
   264→    this.interactTimer = 2000;
   265→    this.interactAnim = anim;
   266→  }
   267→
   268→  setMood(mood, durationMs = 15000) {
   269→    this.mood = mood;
   270→    this.moodTimer = durationMs;
   271→  }
   272→
   273→  /** Get nearby objects and characters within Manhattan distance radius. */
   274→  getPerception(objects, characters, radius = 5) {
   275→    const nearbyObjects = [];
   276→    const nearbyCharacters = [];
   277→
   278→    for (const obj of objects) {
   279→      const dist = Math.abs(obj.position.tx - this.tx) + Math.abs(obj.position.ty - this.ty);
   280→      if (dist <= radius) {
   281→        nearbyObjects.push({
   282→          id: obj.id,
   283→          name: obj.name,
   284→          state: obj.state,
   285→          distance: dist,
   286→          sector: obj.sector,
   287→        });
   288→      }
   289→    }
   290→
   291→    for (const char of characters) {
   292→      if (char.id === this.id) continue;
   293→      const dist = Math.abs(char.tx - this.tx) + Math.abs(char.ty - this.ty);
   294→      if (dist <= radius) {
   295→        nearbyCharacters.push({
   296→          id: char.id,
   297→          name: char.name,
   298→          action: char.action,
   299→          distance: dist,
   300→        });
   301→      }
   302→    }
   303→
   304→    return { objects: nearbyObjects, characters: nearbyCharacters };
   305→  }
   306→}
   307→
   308→// ── Character definitions ──
   309→
   310→export const CHARACTER_DEFS = [
   311→  {
   312→    id: 'maple',
   313→    name: 'Maple',
   314→    personality: 'Warm, chatty barista who takes pride in her coffee. Runs the café downstairs.',
   315→    sector: 'cafe',
   316→    tx: 3, ty: 12,
   317→    colors: { body: '#e8a87c', hair: '#d4543b', outfit: '#f5e6cc' },
   318→  },
   319→  {
   320→    id: 'sol',
   321→    name: 'Sol',
   322→    personality: 'Quiet, bookish regular who loves the window seat. Friendly when approached.',
   323→    sector: 'cafe',
   324→    tx: 10, ty: 16,
   325→    colors: { body: '#b5835a', hair: '#2d5e2a', outfit: '#7ab648' },
   326→  },
   327→  {
   328→    id: 'nyx',
   329→    name: 'Nyx',
   330→    personality: 'Creative night owl who lives upstairs. Paints, cooks, and is slightly scatterbrained.',
   331→    sector: 'apartment',
   332→    tx: 3, ty: 4,
   333→    colors: { body: '#c4aed0', hair: '#3a2d5e', outfit: '#1a1a2e' },
   334→  },
   335→];
   336→

Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_01LiFP6s5rzVHYwTKpqF4dmY",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n     2\u2192// Character System \u2014 Movement, Perception, A* Pathfinding\n     3\u2192// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n     4\u2192//\n     5\u2192// Characters have: id, name, personality, position, action state,\n     6\u2192// mood, energy, facing direction. They move between tiles using\n     7\u2192// A* pathfinding on the walkable tilemap.\n     8\u2192//\n     9\u2192// Walk animation alternates walk1/walk2 sprites while moving.\n    10\u2192// Perception system returns nearby objects and characters within radius.\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192import { MAP, T, MAP_COLS, MAP_ROWS, TILE_SIZE } from './tilemap.js';\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Walkable tiles (everything except walls, fences, water) \u2500\u2500\n    15\u2192const UNWALKABLE = new Set([T.WALL, T.WALL_TOP, T.COUNTER, T.COUNTER_L, T.COUNTER_R, T.WINDOW]);\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192export function isWalkable(tx, ty) {\n    18\u2192  if (tx < 0 || tx >= MAP_COLS || ty < 0 || ty >= MAP_ROWS) return false;\n    19\u2192  return !UNWALKABLE.has(MAP[ty][tx]);\n    20\u2192}\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192// \u2500\u2500 A* Pathfinding \u2500\u2500\n    23\u2192// Returns array of {tx, ty} waypoints (excludes start, includes goal),\n    24\u2192// or null if no path found. Returns [] if already at goal.\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192const NEIGHBORS = [[0, -1], [0, 1], [-1, 0], [1, 0]];\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192export function findPath(sx, sy, gx, gy) {\n    29\u2192  if (!isWalkable(gx, gy)) return null;\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192  const startKey = sy * MAP_COLS + sx;\n    32\u2192  const goalKey = gy * MAP_COLS + gx;\n    33\u2192  if (startKey === goalKey) return [];\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192  const open = [startKey];\n    36\u2192  const cameFrom = new Map();\n    37\u2192  const gScore = new Map([[startKey, 0]]);\n    38\u2192  const fScore = new Map([[startKey, Math.abs(sx - gx) + Math.abs(sy - gy)]]);\n    39\u2192  const closed = new Set();\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192  while (open.length > 0) {\n    42\u2192    // Find node with lowest fScore\n    43\u2192    let bestIdx = 0;\n    44\u2192    let bestF = fScore.get(open[0]) ?? Infinity;\n    45\u2192    for (let i = 1; i < open.length; i++) {\n    46\u2192      const f = fScore.get(open[i]) ?? Infinity;\n    47\u2192      if (f < bestF) { bestF = f; bestIdx = i; }\n    48\u2192    }\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192    const current = open[bestIdx];\n    51\u2192    if (current === goalKey) {\n    52\u2192      const path = [];\n    53\u2192      let c = current;\n    54\u2192      while (cameFrom.has(c)) {\n    55\u2192        path.unshift({ tx: c % MAP_COLS, ty: Math.floor(c / MAP_COLS) });\n    56\u2192        c = cameFrom.get(c);\n    57\u2192      }\n    58\u2192      return path;\n    59\u2192    }\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    open.splice(bestIdx, 1);\n    62\u2192    closed.add(current);\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192    const cx = current % MAP_COLS;\n    65\u2192    const cy = Math.floor(current / MAP_COLS);\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192    for (const [dx, dy] of NEIGHBORS) {\n    68\u2192      const nx = cx + dx;\n    69\u2192      const ny = cy + dy;\n    70\u2192      if (!isWalkable(nx, ny)) continue;\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192      const nKey = ny * MAP_COLS + nx;\n    73\u2192      if (closed.has(nKey)) continue;\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192      const tentG = (gScore.get(current) ?? Infinity) + 1;\n    76\u2192      if (tentG < (gScore.get(nKey) ?? Infinity)) {\n    77\u2192        cameFrom.set(nKey, current);\n    78\u2192        gScore.set(nKey, tentG);\n    79\u2192        fScore.set(nKey, tentG + Math.abs(nx - gx) + Math.abs(ny - gy));\n    80\u2192        if (!open.includes(nKey)) open.push(nKey);\n    81\u2192      }\n    82\u2192    }\n    83\u2192  }\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192  return null; // no path\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Find walkable tile adjacent to an object (for interaction) \u2500\u2500\n    89\u2192export function findInteractTile(objPosition) {\n    90\u2192  const { tx, ty, tw = 1, th = 1 } = objPosition;\n    91\u2192  const candidates = [];\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192  for (let x = tx - 1; x <= tx + tw; x++) {\n    94\u2192    if (isWalkable(x, ty - 1)) candidates.push({ tx: x, ty: ty - 1 });\n    95\u2192    if (isWalkable(x, ty + th)) candidates.push({ tx: x, ty: ty + th });\n    96\u2192  }\n    97\u2192  for (let y = ty; y < ty + th; y++) {\n    98\u2192    if (isWalkable(tx - 1, y)) candidates.push({ tx: tx - 1, ty: y });\n    99\u2192    if (isWalkable(tx + tw, y)) candidates.push({ tx: tx + tw, ty: y });\n   100\u2192  }\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192  if (candidates.length === 0) return null;\n   103\u2192  return candidates[Math.floor(Math.random() * candidates.length)];\n   104\u2192}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Character class \u2500\u2500\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192export class Character {\n   109\u2192  constructor(def) {\n   110\u2192    this.id = def.id;\n   111\u2192    this.name = def.name;\n   112\u2192    this.personality = def.personality;\n   113\u2192    this.tx = def.tx;\n   114\u2192    this.ty = def.ty;\n   115\u2192    this.homeSector = def.sector;\n   116\u2192    this.energy = 100;\n   117\u2192    this.facing = 'down'; // up | down | left | right\n   118\u2192    this.action = 'idle'; // idle | walking | interacting\n   119\u2192    this.colors = def.colors; // fallback render colors\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192    // Movement\n   122\u2192    this.path = [];          // waypoints to follow\n   123\u2192    this.moveProgress = 0;   // 0\u20131 progress toward next waypoint\n   124\u2192    this.moveSpeed = 2.0;    // tiles per second\n   125\u2192    this.prevTx = def.tx;\n   126\u2192    this.prevTy = def.ty;\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192    // Walk animation (4-frame cycle: walk1\u2192walk2\u2192walk3\u2192walk4)\n   129\u2192    this.walkFrame = 0;      // 0\u20133 \u2192 walk1/walk2/walk3/walk4\n   130\u2192    this.walkTimer = 0;\n   131\u2192    this.walkFrameMs = 200;  // ms between frame advances\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192    // Wander AI\n   134\u2192    this.targetObjectId = null;\n   135\u2192    this.wanderCooldown = 2000 + Math.random() * 5000; // start soon\n   136\u2192    this.interactTimer = 0;\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    // Interaction tracking\n   139\u2192    this.lastObjectId = null;\n   140\u2192    this.pendingAction = 'use';\n   141\u2192    this.interactAnim = null;    // 'reading' | 'sit' | 'phone' | null (uses idle when null)\n   142\u2192    this.targetCharId = null;   // ID of a character to face toward upon arrival\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192    // Mood system\n   145\u2192    this.mood = 'content';       // happy | content | neutral | upset\n   146\u2192    this.moodTimer = 0;          // ms until mood resets to content\n   147\u2192  }\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192  /** Sprite key for the current animation frame. */\n   150\u2192  get spriteKey() {\n   151\u2192    if (this.action === 'walking') {\n   152\u2192      return `${this.id}_walk${this.walkFrame + 1}`;\n   153\u2192    }\n   154\u2192    if (this.action === 'interacting' && this.interactAnim) {\n   155\u2192      return `${this.id}_${this.interactAnim}`;\n   156\u2192    }\n   157\u2192    return `${this.id}_idle`;\n   158\u2192  }\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192  get moodEmoji() {\n   161\u2192    switch (this.mood) {\n   162\u2192      case 'happy': return '\ud83d\ude0a';\n   163\u2192      case 'content': return '\ud83d\ude0c';\n   164\u2192      case 'upset': return '\ud83d\ude24';\n   165\u2192      default: return null;\n   166\u2192    }\n   167\u2192  }\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192  /** Pixel X position (interpolated between tiles for smooth movement). */\n   170\u2192  get px() {\n   171\u2192    if (this.path.length === 0) return this.tx * TILE_SIZE;\n   172\u2192    const next = this.path[0];\n   173\u2192    return (this.prevTx + (next.tx - this.prevTx) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;\n   174\u2192  }\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192  /** Pixel Y position (interpolated between tiles for smooth movement). */\n   177\u2192  get py() {\n   178\u2192    if (this.path.length === 0) return this.ty * TILE_SIZE;\n   179\u2192    const next = this.path[0];\n   180\u2192    return (this.prevTy + (next.ty - this.prevTy) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;\n   181\u2192  }\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192  /** Start moving toward a goal tile. Returns true if a path was found. */\n   184\u2192  moveTo(goalTx, goalTy) {\n   185\u2192    const path = findPath(Math.round(this.tx), Math.round(this.ty), goalTx, goalTy);\n   186\u2192    if (path && path.length > 0) {\n   187\u2192      this.path = path;\n   188\u2192      this.prevTx = Math.round(this.tx);\n   189\u2192      this.prevTy = Math.round(this.ty);\n   190\u2192      this.moveProgress = 0;\n   191\u2192      this.action = 'walking';\n   192\u2192      this._updateFacing(path[0].tx - this.prevTx, path[0].ty - this.prevTy);\n   193\u2192      return true;\n   194\u2192    }\n   195\u2192    return false;\n   196\u2192  }\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192  /** Per-frame update: advance position and animation. */\n   199\u2192  update(deltaMs) {\n   200\u2192    if (this.action === 'walking' && this.path.length > 0) {\n   201\u2192      // Walk animation cycle (4 frames)\n   202\u2192      this.walkTimer += deltaMs;\n   203\u2192      if (this.walkTimer >= this.walkFrameMs) {\n   204\u2192        this.walkTimer -= this.walkFrameMs;\n   205\u2192        this.walkFrame = (this.walkFrame + 1) % 4;\n   206\u2192      }\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192      // Advance position along path\n   209\u2192      const tileDurationMs = 1000 / this.moveSpeed;\n   210\u2192      this.moveProgress += deltaMs / tileDurationMs;\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192      while (this.moveProgress >= 1 && this.path.length > 0) {\n   213\u2192        this.moveProgress -= 1;\n   214\u2192        const arrived = this.path.shift();\n   215\u2192        this.tx = arrived.tx;\n   216\u2192        this.ty = arrived.ty;\n   217\u2192        this.prevTx = arrived.tx;\n   218\u2192        this.prevTy = arrived.ty;\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192        if (this.path.length > 0) {\n   221\u2192          this._updateFacing(this.path[0].tx - this.tx, this.path[0].ty - this.ty);\n   222\u2192        }\n   223\u2192      }\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192      // Arrived at destination\n   226\u2192      if (this.path.length === 0) {\n   227\u2192        this.moveProgress = 0;\n   228\u2192        this.action = 'idle';\n   229\u2192      }\n   230\u2192    } else if (this.action === 'interacting') {\n   231\u2192      this.interactTimer -= deltaMs;\n   232\u2192      if (this.interactTimer <= 0) {\n   233\u2192        this.action = 'idle';\n   234\u2192        this.targetObjectId = null;\n   235\u2192        this.interactAnim = null;\n   236\u2192      }\n   237\u2192    }\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192    // Decay mood back to content\n   240\u2192    if (this.moodTimer > 0) {\n   241\u2192      this.moodTimer -= deltaMs;\n   242\u2192      if (this.moodTimer <= 0) {\n   243\u2192        this.mood = 'content';\n   244\u2192        this.moodTimer = 0;\n   245\u2192      }\n   246\u2192    }\n   247\u2192  }\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192  _updateFacing(dx, dy) {\n   250\u2192    if (dx > 0) this.facing = 'right';\n   251\u2192    else if (dx < 0) this.facing = 'left';\n   252\u2192    else if (dy > 0) this.facing = 'down';\n   253\u2192    else if (dy < 0) this.facing = 'up';\n   254\u2192  }\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192  /** Enter interaction state (after arriving at an object).\n   257\u2192   *  @param {string} objectId - ID of the object being interacted with.\n   258\u2192   *  @param {string|null} anim - Animation to play: 'reading' | 'sit' | 'phone' | null.\n   259\u2192   */\n   260\u2192  startInteraction(objectId, anim = null) {\n   261\u2192    this.action = 'interacting';\n   262\u2192    this.targetObjectId = objectId;\n   263\u2192    this.lastObjectId = objectId;\n   264\u2192    this.interactTimer = 2000;\n   265\u2192    this.interactAnim = anim;\n   266\u2192  }\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192  setMood(mood, durationMs = 15000) {\n   269\u2192    this.mood = mood;\n   270\u2192    this.moodTimer = durationMs;\n   271\u2192  }\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192  /** Get nearby objects and characters within Manhattan distance radius. */\n   274\u2192  getPerception(objects, characters, radius = 5) {\n   275\u2192    const nearbyObjects = [];\n   276\u2192    const nearbyCharacters = [];\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192    for (const obj of objects) {\n   279\u2192      const dist = Math.abs(obj.position.tx - this.tx) + Math.abs(obj.position.ty - this.ty);\n   280\u2192      if (dist <= radius) {\n   281\u2192        nearbyObjects.push({\n   282\u2192          id: obj.id,\n   283\u2192          name: obj.name,\n   284\u2192          state: obj.state,\n   285\u2192          distance: dist,\n   286\u2192          sector: obj.sector,\n   287\u2192        });\n   288\u2192      }\n   289\u2192    }\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192    for (const char of characters) {\n   292\u2192      if (char.id === this.id) continue;\n   293\u2192      const dist = Math.abs(char.tx - this.tx) + Math.abs(char.ty - this.ty);\n   294\u2192      if (dist <= radius) {\n   295\u2192        nearbyCharacters.push({\n   296\u2192          id: char.id,\n   297\u2192          name: char.name,\n   298\u2192          action: char.action,\n   299\u2192          distance: dist,\n   300\u2192        });\n   301\u2192      }\n   302\u2192    }\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192    return { objects: nearbyObjects, characters: nearbyCharacters };\n   305\u2192  }\n   306\u2192}\n   307\u2192\n   308\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Character definitions \u2500\u2500\n   309\u2192\n   310\u2192export const CHARACTER_DEFS = [\n   311\u2192  {\n   312\u2192    id: 'maple',\n   313\u2192    name: 'Maple',\n   314\u2192    personality: 'Warm, chatty barista who takes pride in her coffee. Runs the caf\u00e9 downstairs.',\n   315\u2192    sector: 'cafe',\n   316\u2192    tx: 3, ty: 12,\n   317\u2192    colors: { body: '#e8a87c', hair: '#d4543b', outfit: '#f5e6cc' },\n   318\u2192  },\n   319\u2192  {\n   320\u2192    id: 'sol',\n   321\u2192    name: 'Sol',\n   322\u2192    personality: 'Quiet, bookish regular who loves the window seat. Friendly when approached.',\n   323\u2192    sector: 'cafe',\n   324\u2192    tx: 10, ty: 16,\n   325\u2192    colors: { body: '#b5835a', hair: '#2d5e2a', outfit: '#7ab648' },\n   326\u2192  },\n   327\u2192  {\n   328\u2192    id: 'nyx',\n   329\u2192    name: 'Nyx',\n   330\u2192    personality: 'Creative night owl who lives upstairs. Paints, cooks, and is slightly scatterbrained.',\n   331\u2192    sector: 'apartment',\n   332\u2192    tx: 3, ty: 4,\n   333\u2192    colors: { body: '#c4aed0', hair: '#3a2d5e', outfit: '#1a1a2e' },\n   334\u2192  },\n   335\u2192];\n   336\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}
ASSISTANTMESSAGE #35
Now I can see the bug clearly. Let me analyze the walk animation code: At line 201-206: ```javascript // Walk animation cycle (4 frames) this.walkTimer += deltaMs; if (this.walkTimer >= this.walkFrameMs) { this.walkTimer -= this.walkFrameMs; this.walkFrame = (this.walkFrame + 1) % 4; } ``` The issue is that when `deltaMs` is large (e.g., 300ms), the walkTimer accumulates 300ms, which exceeds `this.walkFrameMs` (200ms), so it subtracts 200ms and advances ONE frame. But it should potentially advance multiple frames. The current code only advances one frame per update even if deltaMs spans multiple frame durations. I'm seeing the real problem now — when deltaMs is large, the timer accumulates but the `if` statement only fires once, so it skips frames instead of advancing through multiple frames in a single update. For instance, with a 450ms delta, the timer would hit 450ms, subtract 200ms once, and advance one frame, but it should recognize that 250ms still exceeds the frame duration and advance again. The animation falls behind when frames are slow, but there's something even more subtle going on with how the cycle is structured. Looking at the actual code, the walk animation only ticks when both `action === 'walking'` and `path.length > 0`, which seems correct. But when the path empties out, the character stops moving while the animation might still be mid-cycle, and I need to see what happens at that transition point to understand the full picture. The real issue is that the animation frame advancement doesn't account for multiple frame intervals within a single update call. If `deltaMs` is large enough to span multiple animation cycles, the code only advances one frame instead of catching up to where it should be, causing the animation to lag behind the actual movement. But wait—the stuttering description mentions freezing partway through movement, which suggests the problem might be in the position interpolation rather than just the animation frames. Looking at the movement logic, I'm tracking how the character advances along the path based on `moveProgress` and `tileDurationMs`, and there's something about updating the position coordinates that seems cut off. Examining the interpolation logic more carefully, when the while loop processes multiple tile transitions in a single frame, it updates `prevTx` and `prevTy` to the last arrived tile, then uses those values along with the next destination from `path[0]` to calculate the current pixel position. That should work correctly, but I need to check what the `px` getter is actually doing. The getter interpolates between the previous tile and the next waypoint based on `moveProgress`, which makes sense. But I'm wondering when the action gets set back to idle once the path is exhausted—it looks like that happens when `path.length` reaches zero, which should trigger the idle state. Actually, I think I see the issue now. That idle-setting code is nested inside the walking block, so it only runs when we're actively walking. The real problem might be with the walk animation timer not advancing quickly enough to consume the path entries. Still examining the update method... I'm noticing the walk animation only increments the frame once per update call, which could cause animation stuttering if delta time is large. The real issue might be what happens when `walkTimer` isn't properly reset or when the character transitions between states—if `moveProgress` accumulates without the path being consumed, the character could get stuck in a walking state even after reaching the destination. Let me trace through the `moveTo()` function to see how the path gets initialized and whether there's a mismatch between when movement starts and when the animation updates. I'm realizing the issue — `this.tx` and `this.ty` are discrete tile coordinates, not interpolated positions, so when `moveTo` is called mid-movement and resets `prevTx`/`prevTy` from the current `this.tx`, the character could visually jump backward to the start of the current tile. Let me trace through the movement loop to understand what's actually happening here. The visual position formula shows that when `moveTo` resets `prevTx` to the current tile and `moveProgress` to 0, the character snaps to `this.tx` instead of maintaining their mid-tile position, which would cause a visible stutter. I need to trace through where `moveTo` is actually being called during movement to confirm if that's the source of the bug, or if there's something else going on with the walk animation logic using `if` versus `while`. Looking at the walk animation timing issue: when a frame takes 600ms but the walk frame duration is only 200ms, the `if` statement only advances the animation once per frame instead of three times, causing the animation to slow down during frame drops rather than skipping frames smoothly. The real problem is that leftover timer accumulation from slow frames causes the animation to speed up in subsequent frames as it drains the backlog—so after a 600ms frame, the character might advance frames too quickly until the timer catches up. Let me check the wander AI logic to see if something's actually stopping the character from walking, since the animation timing alone wouldn't cause a complete freeze.
▶ VIEW RAW DATA
